rows.claim: The official Greystar listing shows AC, dishwasher, pet-friendly policy, washer/dryer, garage parking, rooftop pool/deck, concierge, secured elevators, and a private resident entrance.
rows.quote_or_note: Highlights include Air Conditioning, Dishwasher, Garage, Pet Friendly, Pool, Washer/Dryer; community amenities include Rooftop Pool & Deck, Resident Rooftop Lounge, Lobby & Dedicated Concierge, Card Reader Secured Elevators, and Private Resident Entrance.

rows.claim: Greystar discloses several recurring required monthly charges, including pest control, billing admin fee, and SCEP fee, but some utility and lease-cost items remain usage-based or lease-specific.
rows.quote_or_note: Greystar says Total Monthly Leasing Price includes base rent, mandatory monthly costs, and any optional add-ons; the visible table lists Pest Control Services at $3.00/month, Utility Billing Administrative Fee at $4.38/month, and SCEP Fee at $2.83/month, while several utility lines are usage-based.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows strong headline resident sentiment and detailed operational disclosures, but resident review depth is still modest.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows a 4.8/5 blended score with 13 renter reviews and lists Property Manager on Site, Controlled Access, Package Service, Concierge, Doorman, and 24 Hour Access.

rows.claim: The larger Columbia Square campus hosts outdoor events and includes public parking, which raises location-noise exposure for some stacks even if the tower itself is upscale.
rows.source_name: Kilroy Columbia Square on-site amenities page
rows.quote_or_note: Kilroy says the outdoor plaza hosts events including live music and a holiday tree lighting ceremony, and the site has a public 5-level parking garage with valet parking and EV charging.

rows.claim: A resident petition in 2024 complained that valet removal undermined one of the key service promises residents valued.
rows.quote_or_note: The petition says the removal of valet service undermined one of the principal reasons residents chose the building.

rows.claim: Google-review sentiment visible through Birdeye is materially weaker than Apartments.com and includes a serious recent security allegation, but the allegation is not independently confirmed by a higher-tier source.
rows.quote_or_note: Birdeye shows a 3.9/5 rating across 104 Google reviews and surfaces a recent complaint alleging an active-shooter incident involving a maintenance employee and poor building response. This is important but remains anecdotal without a corroborating official report in the retrieved set.

rows.claim: The project was constructed as a Type I concrete high-rise, which is a favorable structural signal for airborne-noise isolation versus lighter wood-frame construction.
rows.quote_or_note: Webcor describes Columbia Square Living as a ground-up, Type I concrete high-end apartment project with 22 levels above grade.

rows.claim: The official site describes The Baxter as an 86-unit, seven-story building with a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking.
rows.quote_or_note: The official page says the building pairs its design with modern amenities such as a rooftop deck, social lounge, and onsite parking.

rows.claim: Apartments.com lists package service, secured parcel room, EV charging, pool, gym, dog park, bike storage, communal working space, and in-unit washer/dryer, AC, and dishwasher.
rows.quote_or_note: Visible amenities include Package Service, EV Charging, Pool, Fitness Center, Dog Park, Bicycle Storage, Coffee Bar in Lobby, Communal Working space, Secured Parcel Room, Washer/Dryer, Air Conditioning, and Dishwasher.

rows.claim: Resident-review signal is currently too thin to treat the high headline rating as durable proof of operations.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows a 4.9/5 blended score with 2 renter reviews, but the visible review section is still very light. That supports interest, not confidence.

rows.claim: Parking and concession disclosures are directionally helpful but still not fully clean or self-contained.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com says parking is available and free of charge for residents, lists garage parking at $0/mo, and notes 1 Month Free, but also says to call for more information on parking policy and fees.

rows.claim: The Baxter is close to core Hollywood daily needs and transit, but that same proximity raises daily street-noise exposure.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com says Hollywood/Highland Station is 0.4 mile away and Musicians Institute is 0.3 mile away, which is great for routine access but places the building in an active Hollywood pocket.

rows.claim: The building is recently delivered, so long-run operational stability and renewal behavior are still unproven.
rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize reported in July 2024 that construction was complete and described an 86-unit building above a two-level, 61-car basement garage. That recency explains why public resident history is still thin.

rows.claim: The official amenities page confirms controlled access/gated entry, EV charging, digital package reception, covered parking, a rooftop sky deck, hot tub, central AC, dishwasher, and in-home washer/dryer.
rows.quote_or_note: The page lists Sky Deck Hot Tub, Sky Deck with Panoramic Views, Controlled Access/Gated, EV Charging Stations, Digital Package Reception, Covered Parking, In-Home Washer and Dryer, Central A/C and Heat, and Dishwasher.

rows.claim: The official pet policy is cat-friendly and dog-friendly with concrete disclosed terms.
rows.quote_or_note: Cats and dogs are welcome, max 2 pets, max 50 lbs per pet, $500 pet deposit per pet, and $50 monthly pet rent per pet.

rows.claim: Skyview Sunset sits at a high-exposure location near Sunset Boulevard and Fairfax Avenue, which materially raises daily traffic and street-noise risk.
rows.quote_or_note: The official site repeatedly emphasizes the location near Fairfax Avenue and Sunset Boulevard and the lively center of the city.

rows.claim: Local development reporting describes the project as a mixed-use Sunset/Fairfax building with retail, which increases exposure to corner activity beyond a pure residential side street.
rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize reported a seven-story project at Sunset and Fairfax with 62 apartments, about 6,500 square feet of ground-floor retail, and 88 parking spaces.

rows.claim: Public operational history is still thin because the building is brand new and has no renter-review depth yet.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com lists the property as built in 2025 with 62 units/7 stories and says there are no renter reviews yet.

rows.claim: The visible listing gives a current available price and concession, but parking detail and some fee details still require direct confirmation.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows a currently available 2BR at $4,295 and says 1 Month Free is available, but also says to contact the property for parking details.

rows.claim: Zillow corroborates the core in-unit essentials and gated access, which helps resolve but not fully eliminate a contradictory Apartments.com FAQ about laundry.
rows.quote_or_note: Zillow lists central AC, in-unit laundry, controlled access, dishwasher, EV charging, cats allowed, and small dogs allowed.

rows.claim: The official amenities page lists dishwasher, stackable washer and dryer, central air conditioning and heating, infinity-edge pool and spa, fitness studio with yoga loft, executive conference center, E-lounge, package concierge, and private access subterranean parking.
rows.quote_or_note: Official page lists apartment and community amenities including dishwasher, stackable washer and dryer, central AC, pool/spa, fitness studio, executive conference center, E-lounge, package concierge, and private access subterranean parking.

rows.claim: The official floorplan page discloses that displayed monthly prices exclude variable mandatory charges such as insurance, utility services, city rent tax, move fees, and other required charges.
rows.quote_or_note: Official fee guide says displayed prices do not include variable mandatory charges required under the lease and that actual charges will appear in the final lease agreement.

rows.claim: Legacy Partners is publicly identified as the operator or manager for 7950 West Sunset.
rows.quote_or_note: The site footer links to legacypartners.com, and Apartments.com identifies Legacy Partners, Inc. CA DRE #01201696 in the property description.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows the property directly at 7950 W Sunset Blvd near multiple shopping centers within a 5-minute walk and highlights a roof terrace, pool, spa, and controlled access.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing places the building on Sunset, notes three shopping centers within 0.3 mile, and emphasizes roof/pool amenities, which supports a strong lifestyle fit but elevated daily activity exposure.

rows.claim: RentCafe shows a small positive resident signal and current leasing activity, but review depth is thin.
rows.quote_or_note: RentCafe shows 4.5 out of 5 from 4 reviews, only 3 available units, and a March 31 move-in special. The signal is useful but still shallow.

rows.claim: Public listings publish concrete application and pet-fee terms: $55 application fee, cat deposit $250 with $25 monthly cat rent, and dog deposit $500 with $50 monthly dog rent.
rows.quote_or_note: Search result snippet for Realtor.com lists application and pet fee terms and confirms dogs and cats are allowed.

rows.claim: The official neighborhood page markets the building as steps from dining, shopping, Sunset Strip, and entertainment, which is a convenience plus but a quiet-living risk for street-facing units.
rows.quote_or_note: Official copy emphasizes nearby dining, shopping, and entertainment on and around Sunset Boulevard; that supports fit for access but also implies consistent ambient activity.

rows.claim: The official amenities page says the rooftop deck has full WiFi coverage and a bird’s-eye view of the activity on Santa Monica Boulevard.
rows.quote_or_note: Official copy frames the roof as a place to work or gather before a night out while looking directly over Santa Monica Blvd activity.

rows.claim: Official 2-bedroom floor-plan content says select units include private patios or balconies facing The Crown’s private interior courtyard.
rows.quote_or_note: This is the clearest public sign that some units may be meaningfully quieter than the boulevard-facing ones.

rows.claim: First Light Property Management is identified on current Crown site materials as the manager.
rows.quote_or_note: The page footer shows manager branding for First Light Property Management.

rows.claim: Apartments.com lists controlled access, key-fob entry, package service, hot tub, fitness center, media lounge, bicycle storage, in-unit washer/dryer, dishwasher, and air conditioning.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing also shows the property was built in 2017 with 48 units over 4 stories.

rows.claim: Resident-review evidence is mixed and thin: Apartments.com shows no renter reviews, while Birdeye aggregates a 3.4-star rating from 14 reviews with both criticism and praise.
rows.quote_or_note: Visible Birdeye snippets include a complaint that the building has only a hot tub instead of a pool and a positive comment praising design and location.

rows.claim: Current public pricing shows active concessions and deposits, but the true monthly number still depends on base-rent versus net-effective math and additional fees.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing advertises 1 month to 6 weeks free on select units, quotes $4,112 to $4,699, shows unit-specific deposits, and says displayed price may exclude mandatory fees or utilities.

rows.claim: Zillow and current unit-level listings corroborate pet-friendly status, in-unit laundry, and published pet charges, but the exact cat terms should still be re-verified on a current lease quote.
rows.quote_or_note: Visible Zillow listing text says washer and dryer are included in every unit and gives pet terms of $500 deposit per pet, $50 dog rent, and $35 cat rent.

rows.claim: The official amenities page lists package concierge, controlled access, fitness center, garage, outdoor pool, chef-grade Viking kitchen appliances, and LG in-home washer/dryer.
rows.quote_or_note: Official building amenities include modern outdoor pool, package concierge, controlled access, fully-equipped fitness center, and garage; unit finishes include dishwasher-adjacent chef’s kitchen setup and LG washer/dryer.

rows.claim: Moss & Company is disclosed as the current management company on Element WeHo’s official site.
rows.quote_or_note: The official page footer states © 2019 Moss & Company.

rows.claim: The official home page says Element WeHo sits right at the intersection of Crescent Heights and Sunset Boulevard with West Hollywood action at the doorstep.
rows.quote_or_note: Official copy emphasizes proximity to Sunset Strip, Chateau Marmont, and surrounding activity rather than insulation from street noise.

rows.claim: The official floor-plan page shows current one-bedroom plus office layouts starting around $4,250 and confirms in-home office or den space, in-home washer/dryer, and walk-in closets.
rows.quote_or_note: Current floor-plan entries include 1 bed plus office layouts priced from $4,250 to $5,550 and larger layouts above that range.

rows.claim: Apartments.com corroborates gated entry, package service, concierge, pool, fitness center, bicycle storage, AC, dishwasher, washer/dryer, and balcony-capable layouts.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing also notes the property manager is on site and highlights views plus office or den layouts.

rows.claim: Pricing is public but the fee stack is still incomplete in self-serve materials.
rows.quote_or_note: Official pages show current base rent bands for floor plans, but a detailed fee guide for utilities, parking, pets, and add-ons was not surfaced in the retrieved public pages.

rows.claim: Zillow snippets confirm pet-friendly status and publish pet terms, but the weight limits appear inconsistent in the surfaced text and need direct confirmation.
rows.quote_or_note: Zillow snippet shows up to 2 cats with $500 deposit and $50 monthly fee, and dogs with $500 deposit and $50 monthly fee, but the visible weight-limit text is internally inconsistent.

rows.claim: The official FAQ publishes unusually detailed fee and policy terms including security deposit range, trash fee, metered utilities, no rent tax, pet fee and pet rent, included tandem parking, visitor parking charges, and renter’s insurance requirements.
rows.source_name: AKA West Hollywood Apartment Residences FAQ
rows.quote_or_note: Official FAQ says deposits range from $2,500 to 2 months rent, trash is $12 per month, water and power are metered, there are no taxes on monthly rent, and one tandem parking space plus valet are included.

rows.claim: The official FAQ confirms a 24-hour resident services team, 24-hour valet, resort-style pool and cabanas, private resident cinema, indoor-outdoor lounge, Wi-Fi-friendly common areas, and specific package-delivery procedures.
rows.source_name: AKA West Hollywood Apartment Residences FAQ
rows.quote_or_note: Smaller packages are logged at the West Tower front desk and delivered outside residents’ doors in two daily delivery windows, with immediate delivery for perishables.

rows.claim: The official home page places the building on the iconic Sunset Strip with a vibrant backdrop, while also describing each apartment as offering a tranquil setting.
rows.source_name: The Apartment Residences at AKA Home
rows.quote_or_note: Official copy supports both sides of the quiet question: premium location on Sunset, but an attempt at calm inside the residences.

rows.claim: Current official site materials identify Korman Communities as the operating company for the annual-lease apartment residences.
rows.source_name: The Apartment Residences at AKA Home
rows.quote_or_note: The official footer states ©2021 Korman Communities.

rows.claim: Apartments.com corroborates doorman and concierge service, video patrol, key-fob entry, EV charging, dog park, washer/dryer, dishwasher, office-ready layouts, and free resident parking.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing also shows 5.0 out of 5 with 2 renter reviews, 24-hour access, and a 9am-5pm office schedule.

rows.claim: RentCafe shows deep resident-review volume with generally strong scores for staff, maintenance, parking, and noise, but not a spotless record.
rows.quote_or_note: RentCafe shows 48 reviews, staff 5.0, maintenance 4.5, parking 5.0, and noise 4.5. Visible comments include praise from a seven-year resident, one complaint that staff is not client-oriented, and a note about a persistent washing-machine issue.

rows.claim: The annual-lease East Tower is part of a larger AKA mixed-use environment that includes retail and serviced residences, which improves service depth but may feel more hotel-like than a standard apartment building.
rows.quote_or_note: The FAQ distinguishes unfurnished annual East Tower apartments from monthly furnished West Tower serviced residences, and the home page notes the shared 8500 Sunset retail plaza.

rows.claim: The official site markets rooftop lounges, meeting rooms, business centers, courtyards with fire pits, and a fitness studio.
rows.quote_or_note: Official amenities page lists expansive recreation rooms with full kitchens, state-of-the-art fitness studios, relaxing courtyards with fire pits, breathtaking rooftop lounges, professional meeting rooms and business centers, outdoor yoga spaces, rooftop spa retreat, and secure underground parking.

rows.claim: The official residences page confirms core in-home essentials including stainless appliances, dishwasher, full-size washer and dryer, smart home technology, and EV charging stations.
rows.quote_or_note: Official page states premium stainless appliances including fridge, range, and dishwasher, in-unit full-size washer and dryer, smart home technology, floor-to-ceiling windows, select balconies, and EV charging stations.

rows.claim: Greystar publishes total monthly pricing language and a detailed required-fee schedule for utilities, SCEP, pest control, and administrative utility billing.
rows.quote_or_note: Greystar states total monthly leasing price includes base rent and mandatory monthly costs, then lists SCEP fee at $2.83 monthly, utility billing administrative fee at $6.06 monthly, pest control at $2 monthly, and usage-based trash, electric, sewer, water, and hot water energy charges.

rows.claim: Apartments.com provides unusually detailed pet, parking, EV parking, package-service, deposit, and situational fee disclosures.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com lists cat restrictions as none, pet deposit $500, pet rent $50 monthly, standard parking $100 monthly, EV parking $175 monthly, tandem parking $150 monthly, package services $3 per occurrence, application fee $35, and security deposit $500 to $1,000.

rows.claim: Third-party listings consistently describe controlled access, key fob entry, package service, EV charging, and secure underground parking.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities section lists package service, controlled access, maintenance on site, EV charging, key fob entry, elevator, lounge, conference rooms, spa, roof terrace, courtyard, and secure underground parking.

rows.claim: Resident-review depth is still thin, which limits confidence in management and quiet operations despite positive headline scores.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows a 5.0 blended score with 2 renter reviews, but the retrieved page also says there are no reviews available to read, so the public resident record is too shallow to treat as strong proof of operations.

rows.claim: The building is very new, mixed-use, and includes ground-floor retail plus rentable amenity spaces, which increases the need to verify day-to-day operations in person.
rows.source_name: LADBS permit record for 1233 N Highland Ave
rows.quote_or_note: City permit record describes a 72-unit mixed-use project over retail and garage, with certificate of occupancy issued on 2026-01-05. The newness helps condition but means there is not much long-term operational evidence yet.

rows.claim: Silhouette is in a highly walkable Hollywood location on Highland, which is convenient but not inherently low-noise.
rows.quote_or_note: Greystar calls the building tucked away among the energetic buzz of Hollywood, while Walk Score is 96 and the site highlights retail, dining, and nightlife access. That supports strong convenience but also moderate ambient activity risk, especially for Highland-facing units.

rows.claim: The official Avalon page surfaces a large current Google review corpus, which is a stronger management signal than the review-light new buildings in this list.
rows.quote_or_note: The official search result for the property shows 4.3 Google Reviews with 231 reviews, indicating a much larger resident feedback base than most newer comps.

rows.claim: Avalon explicitly advertises Smart Access entry and 24/7 digital package lockers, which is a meaningful package and access-control strength.
rows.quote_or_note: Connect+ page says Smart Access lets residents unlock doors through an app or unique code, and package services use digital lockers with email alerts and a unique pin code available 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

rows.claim: Official and verified listing pages align on strong core amenities including in-unit laundry, pool, fitness center, bike storage, furnished options, and on-site retail.
rows.source_name: Apartments.com listing for Avalon West Hollywood
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com says the building offers in-home washer and dryer, stainless kitchens, state of the art fitness center, rooftop terrace, swimming pool, WiFi in common areas, bike storage, furnished options, and on-site retail including Trader Joe's.

rows.claim: The public fee stack is clearer than average, but total all-in cost rises quickly once mandatory Connect+ and covered parking are included.
rows.source_name: Apartments.com fees and policies for Avalon West Hollywood
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com lists Government License Fee $2.83 monthly, Connect+ Smart Tech $30 monthly, Connect+ Internet $81 monthly, cats and dogs each at $500 deposit plus $55 monthly per pet, and covered parking at $150 monthly.

rows.claim: A second large review aggregator also shows a solid but not flawless reputation, supporting a generally strong management score instead of a perfect one.
rows.source_name: Birdeye profile for Avalon West Hollywood
rows.quote_or_note: Birdeye shows a 4.2 rating across 268 reviews, mostly from Google, which broadly backs the official review signal but also suggests some resident friction exists.

rows.claim: The building sits directly on Santa Monica Boulevard with on-site retail and rooftop recreation, which raises persistent ambient-noise risk for a quiet-first renter.
rows.quote_or_note: Official and verified listing pages place the property on Santa Monica Boulevard and highlight on-site retail, rooftop terrace, pool, and a highly walkable WeHo setting. That is excellent for convenience but not a low-noise profile.

rows.claim: There is a dated but material public-safety incident associated with the building, which keeps security risk from scoring low despite strong current access-control features.
rows.source_name: CBS Los Angeles report on 2021 shooting at Avalon West Hollywood
rows.quote_or_note: CBS reported that a woman was shot and killed inside the Avalon West Hollywood apartment building in October 2021. This is not evidence of chronic present-day failure, but it is a non-trivial security datapoint.

rows.claim: Avalon West Hollywood appears established rather than lease-up volatile, but its mixed-use and furnished-housing profile still makes it feel more active than a quieter boutique building.
rows.source_name: Apartments.com listing for Avalon West Hollywood
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com identifies the property as built in 2017 with 294 units over 7 stories and furnished units available. That supports mature operations, but also a larger, more active footprint than a quiet side-street community.

rows.claim: The official marketing explicitly positions the property as being on a quiet street just off Hollywood Boulevard.
rows.quote_or_note: Official homepage says The Line Lofts delivers high-end apartment living on a quiet street off of Hollywood Boulevard in Los Angeles.

rows.claim: The official site confirms unusually strong package, surveillance, and front-desk style operational features.
rows.quote_or_note: Official community features include Parcel Pending automated package storage, Butler Box secure on-site dry-cleaning pick-up and drop-off, 24-hour front desk and courtesy patrol, and 44 surveillance cameras throughout the property.

rows.claim: Core in-unit essentials are strongly supported by verified listing data.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com lists washer and dryer, air conditioning, dishwasher, stainless steel appliances, pantry, walk-in closets, hardwood floors, and loft layouts among apartment features.

rows.claim: Third-party listing data corroborates controlled access, package handling, and surveillance.
rows.quote_or_note: Property services and amenities include package service, controlled access, property manager on site, video patrol, 24 hour access, key fob entry, EV charging, business center, clubhouse, lounge, dog park, covered parking, and 24 hour concierge.

rows.claim: The building is quieter than a boulevard-front address, but it is not inherently silent because the area still reads as busy in third-party noise scoring and remains steps from Hollywood Boulevard.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com gives the property a Soundscore of 67 with traffic and businesses marked busy, while the official neighborhood page says it is just steps away from bustling Hollywood Boulevard.

rows.claim: Public resident-review depth is weak, so management quality is harder to score confidently than the service stack suggests.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com states there are no reviews for this property, so there is little public resident evidence on maintenance follow-through, noise enforcement, or staff responsiveness.

rows.claim: Public pricing is only partly transparent because base rent is visible but parking and pet-fee details are still missing from the main public listings.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows only a $30 application fee, pets allowed, and garage lot or parking contact required, while warning that price shown is base rent and excludes optional and required charges. That leaves all-in cost uncertain.

rows.claim: Live inventory appears inconsistent across sources, which is a mild leasing-operations caution even though it may simply reflect update lag.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows one 1-bedroom unit available, but the SD Property Management availability page snippet surfaces current studio listings instead. That mismatch lowers confidence in real-time availability and leasing precision.

rows.claim: The official site markets coworking-style lobby space, a rooftop pool and dining area, and a fitness center.
rows.quote_or_note: Official amenities section describes an open hotel-esque lobby with creative coworking opportunities, plus rooftop pool, dining, entertainment, and a modern fitness center.

rows.claim: The official availability page discloses that displayed rents are base rent only and that additional fees may apply.
rows.quote_or_note: Page says prices shown are base rent and directs renters to add Essentials and Personalized Add-Ons; it also notes additional fees may apply for package delivery, trash, water, and amenities.

rows.claim: Greystar is publicly identified as the manager on both the official availability page and Greystar listing.
rows.quote_or_note: Greystar listing identifies the property and official site also names Greystar California, Inc. dba Greystar Corp. as manager.

rows.claim: Apartments.com confirms in-unit washer and dryer and air conditioning.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartment Features section lists Washer/Dryer and Air Conditioning.

rows.claim: Apartments.com discloses pet deposit, pet rent, garage parking pricing, and package-service fees.
rows.quote_or_note: Fees page lists refundable pet deposit of $500, pet rent of $50 per month, garage parking at $150 to $200 per month, package services registration at $15, and package services at $3.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows a Busy SoundScore and no renter reviews yet, which limits confidence and points to location noise risk.
rows.quote_or_note: Listing shows SoundScore 62 with Traffic Busy and Businesses Busy, and Property Ratings section says No Renter Reviews Yet.

rows.claim: Urbanize reports the building was developed as a new mixed-use project with 71 apartments over ground-floor retail and a large garage.
rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize project page states 71 apartments with ground-floor retail and 177 parking spaces in a semi-subterranean garage, reinforcing a mixed-use corridor setting rather than a purely residential one.

rows.claim: The official site lists access control, reserved parking, Amazon Hub package lockers, heated pools, rooftop lounge, 24-hour fitness, and EV charging stations.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities list includes Access Controlled Community, Reserved Parking, Amazon Hub Package Lockers, Heated Pools, Rooftop Lounge, 24-hour Fitness Center, and EV Charging Stations.

rows.claim: The official site says apartment amenities include central AC and heat, stainless steel appliances, and washer and dryer in select homes.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartment amenities section lists Central AC and Heat, Stainless Steel Appliances, and Washer and Dryer in Select Homes.

rows.claim: The official resident-testimonial page shows a 3.9 out of 5 score based on 1,495 resident testimonials, with recent praise for leasing and maintenance staff.
rows.quote_or_note: Testimonials page says 3.9/5 from 1,495 resident testimonials; recent reviewed entries mention helpful office staff and prompt maintenance.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows a 4.5 blended score with 14 renter reviews and manager responses.
rows.quote_or_note: Property ratings section shows 4.5 out of 5 with 14 renter reviews and visible property manager responses.

rows.claim: Apartments.com corroborates controlled access, property manager on site, washer and dryer, air conditioning, and dishwasher.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities list Controlled Access and Property Manager on Site; apartment features list Washer/Dryer, Air Conditioning, and Dishwasher.

rows.claim: Apartments.com gives Vantage an Active SoundScore of 70 with busy traffic, indicating noticeable but potentially manageable area noise.
rows.quote_or_note: SoundScore section shows 70/100 with Traffic Busy and Businesses Active, which is better than the most exposed Santa Monica and Sunset frontage buildings but not truly quiet.

rows.claim: Trulia discloses current base rents, cat and dog rent, and pet deposits, while also warning that displayed prices may exclude additional fees.
rows.quote_or_note: Trulia lists base rent, cat rent $45, dog rent $55, cat deposit $500, dog deposit $500, and notes that additional costs and fees may apply.

rows.claim: Birdeye shows a broad 4.2 out of 5 score across 241 reviews, but the visible review sample also includes an older severe complaint about trash-room conditions and leasing-office indifference.
rows.quote_or_note: Birdeye summary shows 4.2 stars from 241 reviews, but one visible older Facebook review alleges roaches in hallways and poor disclosure from leasing.

rows.claim: The official site says Hanover Hollywood is located on Sunset Boulevard in Hollywood, steps from Hollywood and Vine.
rows.quote_or_note: Official site says the community is located on Sunset Boulevard in Hollywood, Los Angeles, steps from the historic intersection of Hollywood and Vine.

rows.claim: The official site markets a 24 or 7 fitness center, resort-style pool, rooftop skydecks, and indoor or outdoor theater style amenities.
rows.quote_or_note: Official marketing lists a state-of-the-art 24 or 7 fitness center, expansive resort-style pool, rooftop skydecks with entertaining spaces, and an indoor or outdoor theater.

rows.claim: The official site says pets are allowed up to two per apartment, with dog breed restrictions and additional fees or deposits that are not publicly itemized.
rows.quote_or_note: Official pet policy says up to two pets are allowed per apartment, some breed restrictions apply to dogs, and additional fees and deposits may apply.

rows.claim: The official floor plan pages say homes include premium appliance packages and smart home capabilities.
rows.quote_or_note: Floor plan page says designer floor plans feature premium appliance packages, smart home capabilities, and spa inspired bathrooms.

rows.claim: Apartments.com lists package service, controlled access, maintenance on site, property manager on site, video patrol, 24 hour access, and key fob entry.
rows.quote_or_note: Property services list includes package service, controlled access, maintenance on site, property manager on site, video patrol, 24 hour access, and key fob entry.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows application, admin, pet, parking, and storage fees, but also warns that some mandatory or optional charges may be excluded.
rows.quote_or_note: Visible fee stack includes $50 application, $50 administrative fee, $75 monthly pet rent, $85 to $225 parking, and $50 to $125 storage, with a disclaimer that fees may exclude utilities or other services.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows a 5.0 blended score and 62 renter reviews for Hanover Hollywood.
rows.quote_or_note: Property rating section displays 5.0 out of 5 with 62 renter reviews, though the parsed page also contains an internal inconsistency about review display that lowers confidence slightly.

rows.claim: Apartments.com gives the location a 67 SoundScore and labels traffic and nearby businesses as Busy.
rows.quote_or_note: SoundScore is 67 out of 100, with traffic listed as Busy and businesses listed as Busy.

rows.claim: BBB shows one customer review alleging a quoted monthly rent changed by roughly $400 within a few hours.
rows.quote_or_note: BBB lists a 1 out of 5 average from one customer review; the visible complaint dated 11/21/2023 says the quoted monthly rent changed by about $400 within roughly two hours.

rows.claim: Urbanize describes Hanover Hollywood as a seven-story, 270-unit mixed-use project with ground-floor retail and basement parking.
rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize project page describes the completed development as a seven-story, 270-unit apartment project with retail and parking.

rows.claim: The official amenities page lists EV charging stations, a controlled access community, and 24-hour concierge service.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities list includes EV Charging Stations, Controlled access community, and 24-hour concierge.

rows.claim: The official amenities page highlights a heated wading pool, outdoor lounge areas, media room, and private yoga studio.
rows.quote_or_note: Official copy says Lumina offers a heated wading pool, outdoor lounge areas, media room, and an exclusive private yoga studio.

rows.claim: The official pet policy says both cats and dogs are welcome with no pet deposits and no weight limits, though breed restrictions and pet fees may still apply.
rows.quote_or_note: Pet policy text says cats and dogs are welcome, with no pet deposits and no weight limits; breed restrictions and pet fees may apply.

rows.claim: The official amenities page lists stacked washer and dryer, Nest thermostat, and garage parking available for additional rent.
rows.quote_or_note: Home amenities include stacked washer and dryer and Nest thermostat; garage parking is available for additional rent.

rows.claim: Apartments.com describes Lumina as a gated building with on-site retail, a heated wading pool, media room, and 24-hour concierge.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com summary says Lumina offers a heated wading pool, outdoor lounge areas, media room, fitness studio, 24-hour concierge, on-site retail, and gated access.

rows.claim: Apartments.com confirms in-unit washer and dryer, air conditioning, dishwasher, and parking fees starting at $100 per month, with deposits based on screening results.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing shows washer and dryer, air conditioning, and dishwasher; fee section shows application fees, deposit options through DepositCloud, and parking fees including $100 per month plus other parking charges listed at $75 to $275.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows a 4.9 blended score based on 4 renter reviews.
rows.quote_or_note: Property rating section displays 4.9 out of 5 with 4 renter reviews.

rows.claim: A Chamber of Commerce listing shows a 4.2 star rating from 131 reviewers for Lumina Hollywood.
rows.quote_or_note: The listing says Lumina Hollywood has a 4.2 star rating from 131 reviewers.

rows.claim: Urbanize reported in July 2025 that Morguard planned to add 36 apartments to the Lumina tower by repurposing vacant offices and parking.
rows.quote_or_note: Urbanize says new housing would take the place of vacant offices and parking at the former Sunset Gordon tower.

rows.claim: Apartments.com gives Lumina Hollywood a 66 SoundScore and labels traffic and nearby businesses as Busy.
rows.quote_or_note: SoundScore is 66 out of 100, with traffic listed as Busy and businesses listed as Busy.

rows.claim: The official site describes El Centro as a self-contained neighborhood on a full city block east of Hollywood and Vine, steps from entertainment, dining, and nightlife.
rows.quote_or_note: Official site says the community sits on a full city block east of Hollywood and Vine and is steps from iconic entertainment, dining, and nightlife.

rows.claim: The current official site footer identifies APT Residential LLC as the operating company.
rows.quote_or_note: Current official site footer shows ©2026 APT Residential LLC and APT Residential California licensing information.

rows.claim: The official amenities page lists night patrol, a fitness center, hot tub or jacuzzi, sauna, yoga or spin studio, private residential parking garage, and a resort-style saltwater pool.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities list includes Night Patrol, Fitness Center, Hot Tub or Jacuzzi, Sauna, Yoga/spin studio, Private residential parking garage, and Resort Style Swimming Pool.

rows.claim: The official amenities page says additional charges apply for some features including parking.
rows.quote_or_note: Official amenity copy says a private residential parking garage is available and notes that additional charges apply.

rows.claim: Apartments.com confirms controlled access, lounge space, sauna, spa, pool, dog park, and a private residential parking garage.
rows.quote_or_note: Amenities list includes Controlled Access, Lounge, Sauna, Spa, Pool, Dog Park, and Private residential parking garage.

rows.claim: Apartments.com confirms in-unit washer and dryer and dishwasher.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartment features section lists Washer/Dryer and Dishwasher.

rows.claim: Apartments.com and Trulia both show cat-friendly and dog-friendly terms, with visible monthly pet fees and deposits.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartments.com shows cat and dog deposits of $100 and monthly pet fees of $50; Trulia also lists dogs and cats allowed with two-pet maximums and breed restrictions.

rows.claim: Apartments.com lists the property as a 2018-built community with 507 units and no renter reviews yet on that platform.
rows.quote_or_note: Property details show built in 2018 with 507 units in 6 stories; the rating section says no renter reviews yet on Apartments.com.

rows.claim: Birdeye shows mixed recent resident feedback, including a complaint that security and the exit gate were major issues.
rows.quote_or_note: Birdeye shows 3.6 stars from 185 reviews; a visible recent Google review says the major issue is security and the exit gate.

rows.claim: Birdeye also shows a recent resident review describing the property as calm, private, and surprisingly peaceful once inside.
rows.quote_or_note: A visible recent review says the neighborhood outside feels lively but once inside the property it feels calm, private, and surprisingly peaceful.

rows.claim: Greystar says the published total monthly leasing prices include base rent and mandatory monthly costs.
rows.quote_or_note: Greystar states that total monthly leasing prices include base rent and mandatory monthly costs, while excluding usage-based and move-in or move-out charges.

rows.claim: Greystar lists abundant parking and EV chargers, concierge service, a dog run, pool courtyard with cabanas, and a fitness center.
rows.quote_or_note: Community amenities include abundant parking and EV chargers, concierge service, dog run, pool courtyard with cabanas, and fitness center.

rows.claim: Greystar highlights pet-friendly homes with washer and dryer included, stainless steel appliances, and floor-to-ceiling double-paned windows.
rows.quote_or_note: Highlights and apartment features list Pet Friendly, Washer/Dryers Included, Stainless Steel Appliances, and Floor-to-Ceiling Double-Paned Windows.

rows.claim: The official site places the building at the crossroads of La Brea and Hollywood Boulevard.
rows.quote_or_note: Official site says the location is at the crossroads of La Brea and Hollywood Boulevard.

rows.claim: Apartments.com lists package service, controlled access, property manager on site, concierge, video patrol, 24 hour access, and a gated courtyard.
rows.quote_or_note: Property services list includes package service, controlled access, maintenance on site, property manager on site, concierge, video patrol, 24 hour access, and gated outdoor areas.

rows.claim: Apartments.com confirms in-unit washer and dryer, air conditioning, dishwasher, security system, and lounge or business-center style common space.
rows.quote_or_note: Apartment features section lists washer and dryer, air conditioning, dishwasher, and security system; shared community list includes a business center and lounge.

rows.claim: Apartments.com shows a 4.8 blended score with 25 renter reviews.
rows.quote_or_note: Property rating section displays 4.8 out of 5 with 25 renter reviews.

rows.claim: Birdeye shows a broader review corpus with lower sentiment and a visible complaint about hot water, elevator outages, and understaffed maintenance.
rows.quote_or_note: Birdeye shows 3.7 stars from 115 reviews; a visible review says hot water was inconsistent, elevators had issues, and maintenance was understaffed.

rows.claim: Apartments.com gives The Avenue Hollywood a 65 SoundScore and labels traffic and nearby businesses as Busy.
rows.quote_or_note: SoundScore is 65 out of 100, with traffic listed as Busy and businesses listed as Busy.

rows.claim: Apartments.com itemizes package-service, pet, parking, tandem, guest-parking, and EV-related charges more clearly than most competing buildings.
rows.quote_or_note: Visible fees include package services at $2.75 per month plus a $20 registration fee, refundable pet deposit of $500, pet rent of $50 per month, standard garage parking at $75 per month, tandem parking at $130 per month, and separate guest or EV parking terms.
